items.prompt: What is the main caveat for anonymization listed in Table 3.5?
choices: It always makes data non-personal permanently
Re-identification via auxiliary data remains possible
It replaces the need for consent
It only works on images, not tables
items.explain: Example 3.13 illustrates that sparse or distinctive records can be linked back to individuals.

choices: Confidentiality in transit and at rest
items.explain: Encryption keeps bytes unreadable without keys; authorized decryptors still need access policy and minimization.
More noise strengthens privacy but can blunt utility if over-calibrated or over-queried.

items.prompt: Federated learning eliminates all privacy risks because raw data never leave devices.
items.explain: Updates can still leak information; secure aggregation and monitoring remain necessary (Example 3.16).
